scope: first-class generated-JSON validator wired into validate.sh strict mode
summary: The existing generated-metadata checks are shallow shell guards registered as warnings. The real schemas exist but validate.sh does not use them. A first-class GENERATED_METADATA_INTEGRITY rule would turn generated JSON into a real completion gate.

title: First-class generated metadata validator
problem: There is only a shallow warning-level validator. GRAPH_METADATA_PRESENT, GRAPH_METADATA_SHAPE, and DESCRIPTION_SHAPE are registered as warnings and check minimal presence. The real graph schema and parser validation exist but validate.sh does not use them for generated-JSON integrity.
evidence: validator-registry.json:184-205, check-graph-metadata-shape.sh:50-64, check-description-shape.sh:45-55, real schema graph-metadata-schema.ts:61-71, parser validation graph-metadata-parser.ts:338-352
proposal: Add a TypeScript rule bridge GENERATED_METADATA_INTEGRITY modeled after SPEC_DOC_STRUCTURE that validates both JSON files through the shared schemas plus stricter semantic checks. Register it as error in strict mode with a grandfather report mode for first rollout.

title: Closed status enum with migration warnings
problem: graphMetadataDerivedSchema accepts any non-empty status string, normalizeDerivedStatus returns arbitrary unknown values, and there is an internal contract conflict: collectReviewFlags recognizes only planned, complete, in_progress, blocked, while tests assert draft and placeholder survive.
evidence: graph-metadata-schema.ts:40-45, normalizeDerivedStatus graph-metadata-parser.ts:168-181, collectReviewFlags backfill-graph-metadata.ts:169, tests graph-metadata-schema.vitest.ts:333-360
proposal: Define one exported enum with explicit aliases. Have the validator fail or warn on anything outside it. Treat current draft and placeholder as migration warnings. Merges with a4-f1 and a4-f4 as the single enum workstream.

title: Packet identity, parent, and children integrity rule
problem: description.json path format is caller-base dependent, parent_id is nullable, children_ids are derived, and the current validator only checks last_active_child_id points to a folder, not exact children membership or child back-references.
evidence: generatePerFolderDescription folder-discovery.ts:884-895, check-description-shape.sh:45-55, parent_id nullable graph-metadata-schema.ts:61-66, resolveParentId graph-metadata-parser.ts:669-679, last_active_child_id check check-graph-metadata-shape.sh:83-101
proposal: In the new validator compute canonical packet id from the folder path using extractSpecFolderFromGraphMetadataPath spec-doc-paths.ts:226-240. Assert packet_id, spec_folder, and specFolder match with no prefix, numeric children have non-null parent_id, parent children_ids matches direct child folders, each child points back, and last_active_child_id is a member.

title: Drift and scope guard for generator runs
problem: Backfill still defaults to the whole specs root, global descriptions.json scans all bases, and drift is not validated: derived summaries are recomputable but validators do not compare stored JSON against derived values. The skipped canonical-save tests are it.fails.skip so they protect nothing.
evidence: backfill-graph-metadata.ts:71-96 and :194-217, ensureDescriptionCache folder-discovery.ts:1153-1178, deriveCausalSummary graph-metadata-parser.ts:995-1007, skipped tests canonical-save-validation.vitest.ts:125-199, soft drift as pass check-canonical-save-helper.cjs:223-250
proposal: Add a dry-run guard accepting --scope that reports every metadata file that would be touched and fails on out-of-scope output unless --global. Add validator checks that recompute description and causal_summary and flag drift. Re-enable the skipped canonical-save tests.
